basic_info.name: Sanya Haitang Bay Surf Town (三亞海棠灣 Surf Town) - NOT VERIFIED as a distinct project. The only existing 'surf town' concept in Haitang Bay refers to Hou Hai Village (後海村), a natural surf village often called 'China's First Surf Village', with a planned 'Surf Tourism Town' (冲浪旅游风情小镇) tourism development - NOT an artificial wave pool. The genuine operating artificial wave pool surf town in Hainan is SURFLAND at Wanning Riyue Bay, not Haitang Bay.
basic_info.location: Haitang Bay, Sanya, Hainan (三亞·海棠灣). The proposed/referenced area corresponds to Hou Hai Village (後海村, also known as Tenghai Fishing Village 藤海漁村), located at the southern end of Haitang Bay near Wuzhizhou Island. No specific artificial wave pool site address exists.
basic_info.operator: [uncertain] - No operator identified for any artificial 'Surf Town' project in Haitang Bay. The Hou Hai natural surf village development is led by Sanya Houhai Tourism Development Co., Ltd. (三亚后海旅游发展有限公司), but this is a fishing-village tourism upgrade, not an artificial wave pool venture.
basic_info.opening_date: [uncertain] - No opening date exists because no artificial wave pool project has been confirmed in Haitang Bay as of 2026-05. The Hou Hai natural surf village 'tourism town' has no published wave-pool opening date.

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: [uncertain] - No artificial wave technology has been announced or installed in Haitang Bay. No PerfectSwell, Wavegarden, Endless Surf, or other brand has been linked to Haitang Bay. SURFLAND PerfectSwell is in Wanning, not Haitang Bay.
technical_specs.pool_size: [uncertain] - No pool exists; no published dimensions. Hou Hai natural beach extends roughly across Queen's Bay (皇后湾), with natural beach break features only.
technical_specs.wave_height: [uncertain] - No artificial wave pool exists. Natural waves at adjacent Hou Hai (the closest existing surf area) typically range about 0.5-1.5 m during the October-April surf season.
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: [uncertain] - Not applicable; no artificial wave pool exists.
technical_specs.wave_types: [uncertain] - No artificial wave types exist. Natural Hou Hai is primarily a gentle beach break suitable for beginners, with a long shallow shelf.
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: [uncertain] - Not applicable; no artificial wave pool exists.

technical_specs.session_length: [uncertain] - Not applicable; no artificial wave pool exists.
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: [uncertain] - Not applicable; no artificial wave pool exists.
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: [uncertain] - No artificial classification exists. Hou Hai (the only existing surf zone in the area) is a natural beach break.

pricing_sessions.price_range: [uncertain] - No pricing exists because no artificial wave pool operates. Natural Hou Hai surf lessons at nearby clubs typically range about 200-500 RMB per session, but this is not an artificial wave pool offering.
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: [uncertain] - Not applicable; no artificial wave pool sessions exist.
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: [uncertain] - Not applicable; no artificial wave pool exists. Board rental at Hou Hai natural surf clubs is roughly 80-150 RMB per session.

experience_guide.best_swell_window: [uncertain] - Not applicable; the project is for an artificial pool, not natural waves. Hou Hai natural swell window is October-April (northeast monsoon).

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Numerous high-end resorts populate Haitang Bay overall, including The Sanya Edition, Westin Sanya Haitang Bay Resort, Conrad Sanya Haitang Bay, and Kempinski Hotel Haitang Bay. Hou Hai Village itself hosts 50+ small inns/B&Bs (民宿) tied to its natural surf scene. Price range roughly 500-3000+ RMB per night.
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: [uncertain] - No on-site accommodation exists because no wave pool exists.
surroundings.nearby_food: Haitang Bay offers resort restaurants and the Hou Hai Village fishing-village dining cluster (seafood eateries, surf-themed cafes, and tourist restaurants). Several Mediterranean and Western surf-cafes operate in Hou Hai catering to surfers.
surroundings.transport: Self-drive, taxi, or didi (网约车) from Sanya. Public bus access via Hou Hai bus stop. From central Sanya it is approximately 60-70 minutes by car.
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX) is approximately 50-60 km away (~70-90 minutes by car). Sanya Haitang Bay Station (高铁站) is closer at about 5-10 km.
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Wuzhizhou Island, Atlantis Sanya, Sanya International Duty Free Mall, Haitang Bay beaches, and the Hou Hai natural surf village are all clusterable in Haitang Bay. No artificial wave pool can be added to this itinerary.

reviews_reputation.pros: [uncertain] - No project exists to review. If referring to Hou Hai natural surf village: stable beginner waves, lively surf scene, abundant inns and surf schools, strong social media presence.
reviews_reputation.cons: [uncertain] - No project exists to review. If referring to Hou Hai natural surf village: extreme crowding in peak season, water quality concerns near village, commercialization, pricing inflation.
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: [uncertain] - No user reviews exist for any 'Sanya Haitang Surf Town' artificial wave pool because no such facility exists. Hou Hai natural surf village reviews on Xiaohongshu, Mafengwo, and Dianping are abundant and generally favorable for beginner surfing.
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: [uncertain] - Not applicable; project does not exist.
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: [uncertain] - No partnerships announced for any Haitang Bay artificial wave pool. The Hou Hai surf-tourism-town development is local government and Sanya Houhai Tourism Development Co. led.

verification.verification_sources: Verification searches conducted 2026-05-15 returned no evidence of a 'Sanya Haitang Bay Surf Town' artificial wave pool project. Sources consulted: (1) China Daily Hainan article on the Kengo Kuma-designed 'Haitang Story' (海棠故事) project for late-2025 trial operation - this is a cultural/commercial complex, not a wave pool: https://hain.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202411/04/WS67286209a310b59111da17df.html ; (2) Ctrip Hou Hai Surf Base guide confirming Hou Hai is a natural surf village: https://gs.ctrip.com/html5/you/sight/sanya61/118822216.html ; (3) Jianzhu Dang'an architecture archive on Hou Hai 'China's First Surf Village': https://www.jzda001.com/index/index/details?type=1&id=2449 ; (4) ThePaper article on Hou Hai surf scene: https://www.thepaper.cn/newsDetail_forward_8720748 ; (5) Sanya Haitang District Party Secretary 21Jingji interview 2025-04-17 mentioning Hou Hai as a 'web-famous surf town' (existing) but no artificial wave pool project: https://m.21jingji.com/article/20250417/herald/6bb5839db9290e06674bc2cc27a95626.html ; (6) Travel Tomorrow / Korea Herald / TropicalHainan / WavePoolMag confirming China's only operating Olympic-standard artificial wave pool is SURFLAND in Wanning Riyue Bay (not Haitang Bay): https://traveltomorrow.com/china-rides-the-wave-with-its-first-purpose-built-surf-resort/ , https://www.tropicalhainan.com/chinas-first-olympic-standard-wave-pool-opens-in-wanning-hainan/ ; (7) Conceptual master plan PDF for Haitang Bay (2018) reviewed - no surf-pool component: http://files.in5.cn/201806/三亚海棠湾概念性总体规划.pdf ; (8) ThePaper 'Sanya new future' article on upcoming Haitang Bay projects (international duty free phase 3 in 2026, Haitang Story phase 2 in 2026) - no wave pool mentioned: https://m.thepaper.cn/newsdetail_forward_32529716. Conclusion: no artificial 'Surf Town' wave-pool project exists or is in construction in Haitang Bay as of 2026-05.
